prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `705a3229`.
codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `705a3229`. The rule is: each nonzero singleton cell grows into an L of the same color, with one arm extending to the nearer vertical edge and the other to the nearer horizontal edge.

I used the Opus hint as a starting point. The Sonnet summary had the right high-level rule but some per-example coordinate prose was inconsistent with the official grids, so I treated that as untrusted and documented the correction in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_705a3229/notes.md). No new shared primitive was needed.

Verification passed end to end: `5/5` official examples, build of `1000` generated examples, and `1000/1000` generated-example verification. The generator stays close to the task family by sampling sparse isolated singleton seeds, avoiding tie rows/cols, and keeping the resulting L-shapes clean and non-overlapping within the official size regime.
